Who's excited? I promise no one was more excited than me. So thank you so much for honoring us by coming. I, gosh, I promised myself I was not going to cry. So that's my goal for the weekend. When I went to my first mastermind event with Russell, at Russell's Inner Circle, one of the guys got up there, and he said, hey, like, I want to just kind of dedicate this to everyone who's new. And he was like, this is the things that I've done to get the most out of all the masterminds I've been to. And at that point, he was like, hey, I want to get the most out of the masterminds I've been to. And he was like, hey, I want to get the most out of the masterminds I've been to. And at that point, he had this little notebook. He was like, I take one notebook to every one of these things. And he's like, in each notebook, I've made $1.5 million from. He's like, I've been to three of them. And I was like, all right, I'm listening to what this guy's going to say. And so what he said was, whenever he has, he takes all those notes, he's been on his blast page, he would write what actions he was going to take off that idea. So we write all the ideas down, which is always fun. And you're like, oh, yeah, I don't want to forget that. But at the end, take that idea that you got from someone at Dave and Buster's and be like, well, what am I going to do? What's the action? Otherwise, it just stays in your notepad and then nothing happens. And so that's how I recommend you do it. So take your ideas, turn them into action items. And then what I do when I do this, and for those of you who've been through with the personal hacks inside of Legacy, I categorize what, basically, is this a lead gen thing? Is this a lead nurture thing? Is this a sales thing? Is this a culture thing? Like, what bucket does this fall under? And then I prioritize them. So going through all of this stuff, especially at the end of the weekend, but as you're going throughout, just to like LN, lead nurture, LG, that way you can come back through and then re-categorize everything, like on the plane flight home, it will take everything here and distilled into actions that you can deposit into your bank account later, rather than just being like, raw, raw ideas. And, like, this is, like, the first time we've been able to, like, generate profit from the gym. It's great. And I was like, why don't you, like, you know, spend more and make more? And all the people there were 8-figure-up business owners. All these guys are big wigs. Alright, I don't know why I'm here, but thank you. And I went up and I gave this huge presentation. I was like, this is all we're doing, this is great, you know, everything's going awesome. And I was like, so we're going to do this supplement thing, and this was, like, months ago. And they were like, so you're spending how much to acquire a customer and what's your lifetime value? And I was like, oh, it's this and this. And they were like, so why don't you just spend more? And I was like, oh. And it was because I just thought that we had grown to the point where I was like, we're making a lot of money and I was like, this is my belief set. And it became real to me of like, everyone, like, and so the next day, I tripled my ad spend, and here you all are. Right? It's real. But you guys have all these, like, what you are to me, there are people in your communities that you are me to them, right? They're like, all these people need your help. And because you're like, we're really content, like, this is like really good and it is. But like, the only reason that you have an impact is twice as many, three times, five times, is literally just because you haven't chosen to yet. It's the weirdest thing. Like, it just became real to me when I had, like, plenty of other people had said it to me, which was like a singe moment, like, people, other people like, spend more money. I'm like, yeah. But like, I didn't believe them because they didn't make as much of that. So I was like, I don't need to listen to them. And then these guys were all like, we make way more than you. Just spend more. I was like, okay. That was my takeaway from this tweet in, was spend more. And what do you know? We made way more money than what I paid them to be there. And so like, let me save you a hundred grand. Like, if you want to make some more money, you can just spend more as long as you have the systems in place. So just, you can stretch your mind on that. Who only a few months ago thought making $80,000, $100,000 plus in a single month was not in the cards at one single gym? So I'll restate my desire. So in short, I want to sell you on the success you can and should have, the beliefs that you used to get in your way. Okay? And to do that, I plan on transferring my conviction over to you and create an experience that allows you to see the success of your peers and have them challenge the paradigms you used to have so you can break through them. Okay? That's why we have the badges the way we do. All right, sound good? Great. Okay. And so here's why I wanted to do masterminds differently. I don't even know if this is technically considered a mastermind, because masterminds typically everyone gets up. I'll explain in a second. But I basically hated masterminds. I really did... I don't really enjoy masterminds as they're typically described, because I feel like they could be so much better. And especially in this industry, I've been a part of a lot of them. And I think the terms are already overused and very, very underdelivered on, which is why some people who are in launch don't go to legacy because they're like, I heard it's just a mastermind. And I'm like, I feel it. I wouldn't want to go either. So we try to make it a little bit better and different. So one, I've been a part of a million of these. And every single time I was always like bored out of my mind at these things. I was like, why don't they do it like this? Like, why don't we get rid of the hot seat where everyone talks about the exact same thing for the first 20 minutes telling you how great they're doing so they can boost their status. And then in the last five minutes, say what the problem is, which is I need to get more customers and then I have the same day to day problems everyone has. Like, the middle 70% of the earners, I was like, I don't need to hear what your day to day problems are. I know what they are already. And I don't need to sit and listen to the first 20 minutes of boasting. It's a field of voice time. So we got rid of hot seat. And so instead we were placed with having the best people talk about what they do best. So some people here have 85% plus show rates. Some people, like the Barnett's who just raised their hand earlier, you guys close, every time you do an internal play, how many people you sign up? Yeah. Internal plays. There are like 100 people on there like internal promos. So before we did a launch in their area, they actually helped me out with the funnel hacking TV launch. They did two, just like one or 10 days of internal pushes for a six week challenge. They're going to run internally only based on referrals. And they just did two large group orientations. They signed up 50 people for a challenge. No ads. Two weeks. They closed everyone but three people walked in their door. And they're all warm referrals. So they're going to talk about how they do their internal promotions. And then we're going to have Ryan and Dave and Logan and all the guys who crushed HTTP talk about how they crushed HTTP. And we're going to have Monica and Gil talk about how they're getting 90% plus to stay on the back end of all their front end offers. It's not that all of them have every single piece of their business, but that piece they've got ironed out. And so like, is that a little bit more valuable than have everyone talk about like themselves for 20 minutes and then five minutes of BS at the end? Okay, is that cool? All right, great. And the next thing I wanted to do, because it is important to give people status, but I wanted to do it a more structured manner rather than just letting it be like while you're just talking. And so the right actions are what should be rewarded, which is bottom line. Most of the masterminds that we've been a part of are how many locations do you have? How much revenue bring in, right? And then you find out, because I talk to all of you, I know, that a lot of you have multiple locations and aren't making any money at all. And I know some of you can say amen to that, but you don't have to now. And so like we're talking about revenue, but we don't have any income, right? And like income is what's going to feed your kids, not your revenue, right? Revenue feeds your ego. So, and the last thing that I kind of did away with like the 15 minute coaching calls that were like monthly, I just, that's what we do daily calls. It's like, if you have anything, jump on, like ask me. You know what I mean? I can't wait a month to get a question answered. Like I wouldn't expect you to. Like if it takes you a month to implement something from a question to question, you'll never win. It just doesn't make sense to me. So that's the way we did away with that stuff, and that's why we have a structure this way. Second thing is that masterminds are typically designed to be the least work for the organizer and the end of, hopefully you don't think that's how we're running our thing. And it's usually the end of the value ladder to extract maximum value. It's a slack adjuster. So it's what they do to try and increase the amount of value they get from each customer so they can afford more on the front end, right? And just to provide access, and it's not really their main thing, it's just something that they just like have something there so that they can get a couple people, you know, 10 people, 20 people to give them 20 grand a year or whatever, and have them say, great job, and then do the whole boasting, hot seat thing. But for me, Jim Lords is the game. Like this is the main game. Like this is my EFT. Jim Launch is my six week challenge. Jim Legacy is my EFT, right? If you think about it the same way. I structured this business exactly the same way as I structured the gyms. We have a front end offer. You meet at a halfway point for a call. We give you an abandoned hope sale that's an irresistible offer. Like notice the theme here, right? Irresistible offer to get you to stay. Like I'm doing the same things here. And I realized the other day I was like, wait, on our onboarding call, Jamie, I was like, are you telling them that the halfway point is a mandatory call? And she's like, oh, I didn't do that yet. And I was like, dang it. Tell them it's mandatory. But we do the same thing. But this is my game. Like this is the whole game because for me, I can't remake an industry and give everyone their gym back and give clients way better outcomes with only 20 people. Right? We do it differently. Okay. Sound fair? Okay. Sweet. So next up, what's up with all these ribbons, right? Who's got ribbons? Have we got some ribbons? Okay, sick. So I totally stole this from Funnel Hacker, but it's awesome. And the biggest thing that I can tell you is be really, really honest when you're grabbing those ribbons because like the only person who really knows is you. And I remember, so for me, my little two comic-cub story was last year I went to Funnel Hacking Live and I qualified for a two comic-cub award. But the business wasn't where I wanted it to be. And they were like, you qualify? And I was in the inner circle. So they were like, we know you've done over a million sales. And I was like, I know. I didn't feel right about it. Like I didn't want to go up there and like cheese and stand here and be like, I'm so successful and like secretly feel like I'm a fraud. You know what I mean? I didn't want to feel that way. And so I felt so ashamed that I didn't win. You know what I mean? That I told Layla, I was like, we're getting four of those things next year. I was like, excuse me. I was like, we're getting four of those next year. And but like if you feel short, it's important to be honest with yourself because like, let that drive you. You know what I mean? Like don't lie to everyone here because like we don't feed your kids. You know what I mean? Like you do. And if anything, if you feel insufficient, let that drive you. And I wanted to make this public so that you could kind of have that nasty feeling of that's where you're at. I wanted that because that's like what drove me, you know, and I want that to drive you. And I also think that this should be a safe place so you can actually get cheered on for your great work. And so that if you don't know it because everyone here doesn't know everyone, right? And if you see, oh, this guy's a level five at sales, I suck at selling. You can immediately start talking to those people. And so it kind of helps you sift through and just like get better quality conversations. I thought this was pretty cool. Is that cool? Okay, sweet. So here's how it works. All right. There's two different sides to this and it's because what we've realized at Gym Launch is the business's growth will be straddled by our ability to grow personally. And then also our team's ability to grow personally because at a certain point like it's no longer us that has to, I mean, we still have to grow. Oops. Bang a microphone. We still have to grow as business owners, but then the people who are underneath of us also have to grow. Otherwise, because they have to continue to grow with us at the same rate. Otherwise, the business will stop growing until they grow. And so personal growth is so important to me because it literally will make you more money. So they're really interrelated, but it was important to delineate the two. The first one was I saw an explosion in my income when I started tithing. There are some people who are non-believers who have also experienced the same thing. In Dink Kennedy's book, Wealth Attraction, he talks, he's like, I don't know why it works this way. He's like, you think that you'd make $100 and you give 10 away and now you have 90? He's like, but what really usually happens is like you give 10 away and then like 90 goes times four and you're up like 360%. I don't know why it works. All right. I don't. But I just obey. So we've been doing that and basically since we started tithing, everything changed for us financially. I think part of that is also you're just aware of what you make and you start like taking an income. The second big milestone that I know a lot of you guys are striving towards, Josh Pond is paying down $8,000 a month on debt besides paying himself is becoming debt free. And one of the important measures of that is having, is measuring your net worth because sometimes it can feel really discouraging when you pay down debt. Anyone feel discouraged when you pay down that? You say this huge thing, you're like, I'm never going to pay this down. Right. You just eat it one chunk at a time. And the thing is, if you're measuring net worth, which includes debt, you'll see that number go up. And so if you measure that number, like I know like, where are you at? Layla somewhere. Anyways, like the first thing I do literally every morning, it's probably obsessive. I take my phone out and then I update our net worth tracker every morning. It's probably not good. But like every billionaire that we've heard of does this. And I was like, well, if they do it, maybe I should do it. And so that's why there's six levels of basically financial tracking for you. So you can see what level you're at. So right now you don't track your net worth. That would be level zero. So you wouldn't get that ribbon. But hopefully next time you'll be at, you know, level two or three. Right. And the reason that there's level two, which is like Josh would be level two because he's still got debt, but he's paying down his net worth is going up by 5,000 a month because he's actively paying debt down every single month. Right. Next milestone, Logan hit. Where are you at? Logan. Yeah. So Logan had debt when we met, right? And then you had no savings. I know I took your last savings. But I mean, and we started in April and now besides all of that stuff, he just crossed his 100K net worth mark. Right. That's sick. That's awesome. And like, that's the stuff that I want to be applauded rather than like, we're doing 40K a month. It's like, we're taking home three, but you know, whatever, you know, like, you know, it's just like, I have seven locations, but we can't pay our mortgage. Like, I don't want like, I don't want to say you don't deserve status, but like, I think what happens is like, you just start feeling like a fraud when you get that status. Because when you get home, you feel empty. You're like, ah, that wasn't, I felt like I was lying. And I don't want to create that environment. So we do it this way. Next thing is, these are kind of like the health one. It should be like health colored, like red or something, but whatever. Anyways, so it's basically all of the things that we had to start doing. And as you grow in the business, you have to grow by learning new things, but you continue to grow by continuing to do the things that you did before. So if you like, started to get in shape, and you were a really fit person, and then you started to gym and then stopped being in shape, right? Anyone? You need to keep doing that. Because ethically, you know, like, you can't sell someone all day on the fact that they need to prioritize the health and like, eat better and work out every day, and you don't like prioritize your health or eat, and like, I'm really busy. I'm a gym owner. They're like, well, I'm really busy. I have a kid and, you know, so billionaires work out. They have time, I have time. You just make time. But like, you have to prioritize it because like, you will, because like, one of the big realizations that we've had is we've grown is that, like, units of time are measured in effectiveness and output. Not in how much time you work, right? Which I probably think is one of the points I'm going to make in a little bit. But when you work out and you eat well and you're like, well, hydrating, you think better, you do more work, and you do better work. I know this sounds like, I like, this is like singe moment stuff. I heard this stuff a million times, and then I started doing it and we started making more money. So I'm just telling you this stuff because like, it actually worked. Alright, cool. So, and then the last one is this gold ribbon which some of you guys will get to, and that's kind of like the aspirational goal I want for everyone, which is that your passive income is greater than your expenses. Which in the business, oops, in the business context, thanks mom. Anyways, the business context, the golden ribbon is, the golden ratio is achieved, which is the percentage of people who refer to your business is greater than the percentage of people who leave your business every month. Right? So the reason that Chipotle grows, and doesn't really need to run ads, is because Chipotle is so good that if someone comes to me and they're like, I've never been to Chipotle, I'm like, stop, let's go. Like, I'm not even hungry, let's go. Like, let's let, come on. Like, I want to be there for this experience for you. You know what I mean? But like, imagine if someone's like, well, I don't really go to a gym, they're like, you haven't been to Kyle's gym? Are you serious? What are you doing after work, like today? Like, you're coming to my gym. They can talk about your business like that, you don't need ads, right? And so that's my goal for everybody. That's the big, that's the golden ratio. Because then at that point, you need nothing. You grow virally. Right now, we have more people, like right now we have, the reason we've grown like this, obviously we're good at client acquisition. But we get about 60 applications a week from gym owners off of referrals. Right? Because we really focus on doing a really good job. Hopefully you feel that way too. But, okay, so I'll go over these really briefly. Level one for EFTs, that's all EFT stuff. Add a new price, because I know some of you guys have legacy EFTs. I want to applaud the people who have made the change, switched over the price that they now know is a profitable price to run their business at. Okay? One, two, three, four. Closing, so this is for the salespeople. So people who are like, like, Monica the Calgary Cannon, she's probably a level four salesperson, because she only crushes it. Like, she's very, very shy. She's right there in the red. You can go harass her. The Calgary Cannon close was added to the sales training after Monica. She did like 70 grand off of an internal promotion for HCP. She started at $30,000, price dropped them all the way down to three, and closed like 19 people in the room. Crushed it. She was like, this is what Kim Kardashian's program was. Now, I can't say I can donate, but I do not hate on it. It was so good. It was such good pitching. So, like, if you have someone who's struggling with sales, talk to her. You can see it. She's level four, right? The next thing is outsource. So the blue one is time. It's kind of like where are you at with your time? So if you're still training sessions, you don't get one of those yet, right? And that was our one year anniversary. I just sent one email. I was like, hey guys, we're doing it. I'm just like, we're just going to hang out at the gym. And you know, you guys can come if you want. And like everyone showed up and cried a lot there. There's my comic relief. Good. This is my second location. And this one I was like, you know, trying to a little more ego. I was like, this one's 7,000 square feet, about a quarter million on the build out out of pocket. And then I learned my lesson because that one didn't make any more money than this one did. And that's why I tell you guys, don't do that. This is like, I'm telling you guys, like, don't make my mistakes. So this is the fourth location we have. This one I bought. I just bought it outright for 20 grand and I did it off of cash flow. So I said, hey, I'll pay 20 grand for it. I was like, but let's do it over time. I was like, I'll just take the gym over. I was like, and then we'll just, we'll just do it. And I signed up 100 people in the first three weeks. And I was like, great, this was awesome. And so I found out that it's a lot easier to acquire an old gym that's starting to win. Don't do that unless you have cash. But like, I've learned these lessons. And so, you know, we guys asked to speak at Phonac or two years ago because that was actually, I sold, it's like, I sold all my gyms one year ago. Like, this is all like recent, you know, like, I started this business like January. And so two years ago I spoke and he was like, you're crushing with all this local stuff. Like, can you present it? That's what we talked about. And then my mom got sick and everything changed. She's okay now. So I had to put that in there. She's okay now. But everything changed for me. So I had to fly home. I was home for seven months. And before I did, I figured I was like, well, I'm gonna be home because she's sick. Like, I'm like, I'm gonna go, you know, be with her. I reached out to some old friends and connect. And one of them, she's been this guy. Where's Josh? Where are you at? There he is. And see, it's a Baltimore thing. He's the only other person like, where in backwards hat here? I just have hair that gets everywhere. But Josh allowed me to twist his arm into letting me make him money on my own dime and time. I love telling this story. So basically, I called him up. I was like, or a friend of mine said, hey, this guy's struggling with his gym. I know you're good at all this marketing stuff. Like, can you help him out? I was like, yeah, sure. Why not? I'm in the area. Like, I want to take my mind off of stuff that's going on with my mom. Like, I'll have nothing to do. Anybody who says like visiting hours with, you know, like family, you're like, you have this much time and like all the rest of the time, you're just like worried. Right? So I was like, well, I might as well like do something. And so I was like, I'll help this guy out. And I called him up and I just flown in and his partner, which he's no one partnered with, was like, yeah, we don't want you to do this. I was like, I'm here. Like, we're doing this, right? Like, we have to do it. And they're like, no, like we have 70 clients. We don't make any money and we can't take any more. And I was like, I was like, why? They're like, if the classes get bigger, they can't do double unders. And so I remember, maybe you remember this moment, I like picked up the jump rope and I was like, this is why you're going to be broke. Right? Like, this is it. Like, you're allowing this jump rope and double unders to be the reason that you can't feed your family. Right? And I was like, if you have a full facility and it is not profitable, I was like, you see the problem with the business model here, right? And so finally, it took me four hours to convince them to re-let me spend my own money and spend my own time selling people into their gym. They finally let me do that. And so within 10 days, we signed up, 70 new clients at his gym. And they had 70 total at the time. So we doubled his gym in like a week and a half. That was exciting, right? That was cool. Right? Okay. And so that was the birth of gym lunch. I was doing it because I was kind of like, I had nothing to do and I wanted to keep my mind occupied and I was like, huh, that was cool. We should do more of that. And so then we tested it like myself and a couple of their markets. I flew around because I wanted to make sure it worked other places. And then I flew back home to see if anyone else could do it. My mom is now, she's better now. And that's when Layla decided to quit her job and go all in on Alex. Boom, sold her. I sold her way harder later. Alright, I'll tell you another story. But she quit her job and then she came to try this out with me. So she started, because I was like, okay, I can do it, but can someone else do it? So she did it. But then I was like, okay, well, she's obviously really invested in me. Can someone who's not like trying to be with me like do it? And so then we had someone else do it and it worked again. And so then at that point we decided to hire and train six sales guys. And we started Jim Lowe in January of last year. And at that point we were doing six to eight gyms a month that we would fly guys out to. I'm telling you this is sort of, when we have all this trial and tested stuff, it's because we flew guys out and we tested it in the bazillion markets before we even made a product. One of the other reasons that a lot of other products that you've bought haven't worked is because they actually didn't test them. So we did 28 markets over four months with six to eight guys. And we were bringing in tons of revenue, but the overhead was higher than our business is now. So the overhead of then is higher than business is now. We make a lot more money now. And it was really hard on the sales team because these guys were gone for 21 to 24 days a month. And then they'd fly back for seven days to be home with their families and then fly out again. So I could see the dream on the team. I was like, this is an escalable model. And so I decided to get out of the gym business altogether. This was April of last year, like eight months ago. Crazy. And so we decided to get out and start an online weight loss brand with my wife being the face called Queen, which now most of you guys refer to as HTTP. And so it's like, what sometimes you think, like you see your journey and you're like, man, how did this even happen? Like, this is how it happened for us. And so we started selling a thousand day of online programs. And that's why all those emails and all those things look so clean and beautiful in the HTTP program. And I was like, huh, how about that? That was nifty. And then I called up the second guy and same exact conversation. He was like, how much? And I was like, X plus a little bit. And he was like, yeah. I was like, huh. On the next day I and like eight guys later in one day all said yes. And I was like, holy shit, excuse me. And I was like, dang. So like pretty cool, right? So things weren't always good. And I'm going to circle back to why I'm bringing these up because I told you that what I wanted you to take away was the principles from the failures rather than making the failures for yourself. Okay. So like, let me save you more money in the next 20 minutes, then you'll pay me for the next three years. I've eight failed partnerships over this time period. I just love people. I was super depressed and I got to the point where the gyms were really, truly running an autopilot. And I, like I would call them, I call my manager and say, you guys need anything? I was going through like three bottles of black label a week when I met Layla. And it's because like, I mean, we're entrepreneurs, like, I mean, we're still making money. Like, I had no, like, there's no consequences. I was like, I'm just gonna get drunk all day, whatever. Living the life. Well, because everyone talks about freedom, but all my friends had jobs. Who am I gonna hang out with? Like, all my entrepreneurial friends are working. So I was like, I don't know. I just drink a lot. And so I stopped working out for a really long period of time and only eating fast food. I just have really, like what Trevor says, potato chip genetics, like, I don't, I can eat any push-ups and I'll still have a six pack. It's crappy. Well, for everyone, like, for me it's great. So anyways, I was able to still look fit while this happened, but I was like, drinking every single day and just eating fast food and not working out. And at this point, this is like, right as my mom started getting sick, my grandfather died and I was really close with. Right two months prior to her getting sick, got a DUI. The last partner I had took all of the money from the business. So I took, so I sold the gyms and all of the cash payments that I got, I put into the next business and then he drained it, sent it to his girlfriend in Sweden and then followed Bankruptcy. So, like, I started to zero. Like, I get it. Very frustrating. And I almost had to follow BK myself in November of last year. So like, stuff can change really fast. And so why do I share this? Because humans are the only animals who learn from the mistakes of others. Right? And the value is not in the experience. I don't want you to have the experience. It's in the improved decision making process and the principle that the mistake instills. That's the value, not the mistake. Right? And so failure-breed success. I borrowed this from Ray Dalio's book, Principles, somebody has probably seen it. I thought the first part of the book was way better than the rest. I got bored at the end. But I thought this was like, it was so well visualized. Ray Dalio owns the most, the biggest hedge fund in the world. So his hedge fund manages like a trillion dollars. He's like a billion, like 26 billion. Very, very wealthy dude. Knows a lot about money and stuff. Anyways. But failure-breed success because it creates superior decision making processes. Right? So that's kind of the growth. That's how people grow. Right? And so who's here has ever said, if I only know what I know now. Right? Right? Where's Steve Bowser? Where are you at? There you go. Okay. So if I had met you when I was 12, like this could, you know, you could have started your gym. He was saying this last night. It was just, it was an inside joke. It could have been there. Anyways. So for knowledge workers, which is what most of us are now or should be after you stop training, training sessions, who wants to stop training, training sessions, everyone know what that? Okay, cool. The value is not measured in units of time, but units of effectiveness. That's what I was talking about earlier. And so if you, we are the metaphorical acts, the sharper you become, the more trees you can cut down, except you shouldn't view the evolution of this from that acts to this acts, but more so from that to that. It's a stretch in thinking. You know what I'm saying? Like it's not like that's incremental improvement versus like the quantum leaps in improvement that you can have when you adopt new principles. All right? Because if the amount of effectiveness and output of the knowledge worker, like a billionaire, like Jeff Bezos, I read a lot of biographies on billionaires, can do compared to a normal warehouse worker isn't 10X. He's not like 2X as effective as a warehouse worker who works at his warehouse or 10X. He's millions of times as effective. Like when he pushes, he moves mountains. He changes industries, right? And so the effective output of his time is because he's not incrementally sharper. He's quantum leaps sharper, right? And so if you can buy those principles, right, how valuable is that? And so our goal has always been to go all in on becoming the types of people who can do the things that allow us to have the reality we want, right? Be do have. It's biblical. That's not like, I would love to take credit for that. That's just Bible. Be do have some of you guys have heard that. Right? And that's like one of those singe moment things. Like I heard it so many times until I realized that like we actually had to become the types of people who would be able to do the types of things that would give us what we wanted, right? I think God makes it that way on purpose because he doesn't care about what we have, but he puts the carrots there so we can change who we are, right? So we guys don't believe that's fine. But the concept still stands. Okay. And we do that by voting with our dollars about the things that we care about becoming better at acquiring skills and acquiring principles to make us better so we can get the things that we want, right? And so the best investment in the world, if you think about it, like if you could bet, would be investing in like a billionaire who lost all his money legitimately, not like the Bernie Madoff, not like him. The good ones, right? Because like we lost everything a year ago. We got it all back and some, right? And so it's the principles that you have because what it does is it builds bottom stories in your building, right? The better you become, the lower you fall. Like you don't hit the ground anymore. And if you do, you have all of the stills to come right back up and crush it again. And so it's just, it's a temper, like if I could bet on millionaire horses, I would. That would be the best bet ever because they're going to make the money back way more, right? And so you can become like, if you want to, like if I think about this from a security standpoint, if I want to secure my future, the only thing that a government can't take away because I have parents who, whether the revolution in Iran, so they're really big on investment in education. Yeah, really big on that. So thoughtful fun experiment, sorry, fun thought experiment. How much would it be worth to buy Warren Buffett's decision making? Like if you could buy his ability to make decisions, right? Trillions, right? And I used to think about buying experiences or battle scars, but experiences doesn't always breed success, right? So how many people have someone in their family who keep making the same mistake over and over, dating the same type of guy? Or like going back to drinking, whatever it is, right? Keep, like keep doing that dead end job they hate, whatever it is, right? They keep making the same mistake over and over again, which is why failure doesn't necessarily breed success. It's like, I just want to separate that. You don't need to fail in order to succeed, right? You can just buy the principle and then hack it. Like that's how we've done it, right? And so experiences do not equal improved decision making process, right? That's for Warren's thing. So why don't I bring all this up? Because my goal is to have you walk away from this with that, learn principles without that. You don't need the battle scars. You've got probably already have enough scars, right? Do we have enough scars? Do we have a few less scars? Cool. So here's another question for you. How long did it take you to, from the time you started Gym Launch, I'm just saying the first thing because some of you guys are just getting off of that. And so you started making money. How long did it take? A week. A week? Right. A couple of days, a week, two weeks, right? How did it happen so fast? Because you got that without the bazillions of wasted time and effort that I went through to build it, right? And so it worked on the first try. It's pretty cool, right? And so with this preface, I want to revisit those mistakes that I was talking about earlier, okay? So my fantastic F-OPS. So let's look at them under the principles microscope so I can save you a bunch of money, right? And time. So what principles did I take from these that have made me more successful, all right? My field partnerships. I have an increased understanding of partnerships and legal and how 99% of the time you don't need partnerships and how to do them the right way. Usually marry the person. That's what I had to do. But a lot of times you don't really need a partner. Like the worst partnership in the world, and I know some of you guys are in here so just don't get weird about it. But if two people have the exact same role, like we're both interested in fitness and we both training, you both fulfill the exact same need for the business. And if a lot of times, well, I had an early investor, a lot of times it's better to just have a bank because you only have to pay back this much rather than give half your business away, right? If you always think about everything as an affiliate relationship or an associate instead, which is how we've shifted all of our thinking, then what it does is it keeps everything in an equal exchange. So like if it ever gets to a point where like affiliates don't want to send people to us, they can stop, right? And so we're going to try really hard on our side to make it worth it for them, right? And so it keeps equal exchange. But I'm sure some of you in this room have either been in partnerships or are currently in partnerships or as Josh just exited his partnership, where you probably feel like you might be doing more of the work, right? Now mind you, both partners probably feel that way. So it's just, it's being really, really mindful. And so now like we basically, it's, I don't foresee us doing a partnership in any future time period, you know, really I can think of. Because there's just, there's so many things that you can buy rather than partner. And if you want something else, you can usually do affiliate or associate relationships and not have to risk that stuff. So that's, this is Alex's minted principle from this failure. Me almost finally bankruptcy, which is why I don't go crazy with money, okay? I see some of the stuff that you guys are buying with some of the money you guys are making and like, I love you to death, but some of you guys should not be spending that money. And I focus so hard on income and net and superior growth and revenue and a very cautious partnerships because of that, because I almost thought bigger. Like I know what it's like to be at like absolute zero and I don't want people to go through that and a lot of times like we spend less. So I pulled our last, I was going to put it on here, took it off. Our last four months I pulled our debit card transactions. Right, like, so we were like, man, like my overhead's going up, as my revenue goes up, my overhead is more than yours is. Like, this event was probably more than a lot of, like some happy guys like, whatever, it's a lot. But like, it's always gonna be like that. Like, it will always go up together. And so, but that means is that the pressure goes up as you make more, because you have to perform at a higher level consistently, you can't have off months, right? Because if you have an overhead of, let's say, a million dollars a month, like, you mess up, you're not like zero, you're down a million, down a million the next month, right? Like, that adds up fast. And so, that's why you have to do the things like, exercise regularly, eat well, sleep. Because you can't have those off days, right? I'm giving you this stuff because it took me a lot of failures to figure this out, and I'm telling you because these are like singe moments where I'm like, man, I wish I had figured this out because I'd heard this earlier and I didn't act on it. And I suck all my savings from my super successful gym into a second one, which I really didn't need to do, because it didn't make any more money than the first one did. And I got the third one for one-tenth the cost. And again, that one made the same money. So, if you have that, don't make that mistake, okay? And since we're going through a lot of stuff, I'll suggest you divide your notes into these five core functions, okay. Which is lead gen, lead nurture, sales, fulfillment, and resell, okay. Right now, if you can write those on your notes, give yourself a quick rating from one to five, so that you can pay attention more during the ones that are on the one to three range, rather than your four to five range. This is the first thing I did in our business assessment when we were looking at our stuff, is where's the biggest leak in our pipe? Because these are the things, like, you'll often get a lot more from fixing a leaky pipe than changing the pipe from copper to aluminum, right. I don't know what the plumbing, copper to gold, whatever. But you get my point here, is that, if you're already a sales genie, we like to work on our strengths, which is fine. But your business will grow a lot more from fixing the weaknesses. Which is not necessary like, man, yes, I'll leave it there. You do need to get better at everything. But in terms of like short things, if you're like not getting people in the door, fix that first, and then you'll be able to fix everything else, okay. So, you have to hone in on the gats and opportunities you see in the pipeline. Try and connect with those, that's why the ribbons are there. You have strengths that are your weaknesses, okay. If you don't have strengths, gain the conviction from others like you who have overcome those bottlenecks and learned from them. So, if you're like, I suck at sales, go find people who sucked at sales and got better at it. Like, talk to Chris Barnett and Robin if you have a crappy show rate and want to get it better.
